day_notes: First strikes — $5.6B munitions in 48 hrs (WaPo)
Sustained air campaign — 900 targets in first 12 hours
First trading day — 3 carrier groups fully engaged
Hormuz formally closed — insurance-driven shutdown
Minab school — global scrutiny; Pentagon: $11.3B cumulative
Pentagon reports $11.3B to Senate — confirmed anchor point
One week — WTI best week since 1983
Oil infrastructure strikes begin — Kharg, Abadan
Brent intraday $119.50 — SPR release announced
New Supreme Leader — 16 minelayers destroyed
IEA 400M bbl reserve release — CSIS: $16.5B cumulative
Brent closes above $100 — 5 vessels hit in Hormuz
KC-135 crash kills 6 — IDF ground war in Lebanon
Kharg Island struck — 15,000+ targets — AUMF debate
Weekend — France/Italy open talks with Iran
Weekend — gas at $3.69 — war cost at ~$20B
Day 17 — Dubai airport struck; Brent $106.18; 56% oppose war
Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers +1.5%; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 19 — South Pars struck; Brent $108.43 (+52.7%); 3 officials killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; IMO: 3,200 stranded; ~$22.5B
Day 20 — Iran retaliates: Ras Laffan, SAMREF, Kuwait hit; Brent intraday $119.11; Fed holds 3.50-3.75%; $200B supplemental requested; ~$23.4B
Day 21 — 7,800+ targets; Quadruple Witching; S&P new low below 200-day MA; Brent $108.19; gas $3.91; 4 officials killed; Boxer deploys; ~$24.3B
Day 22 — Nowruz/Eid; 8,000+ targets; 130 vessels destroyed; Natanz struck; Diego Garcia attacked; Dimona hit; 9 salvos at Israel; ~$25.3B
Day 23 — Trump 48-hr ultimatum; Iran threatens regional energy destruction; Cooper: 'Iran declining'; IDF wave in Lebanon; Tripoli arriving; ~$27.3B

Day 24 — Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days; claims 'productive conversations' via Oman/Turkey/Egypt/Pakistan; Brent crashes 13.6% then recovers; S&P surges +1.7%; IDF strikes Tehran; Iran strikes Netanya; gas $3.96; ~$28.3B
Day 25 — Iran strikes Tel Aviv in 7 waves; CENTCOM: 9,000+ targets, 140+ vessels; Tripoli enters CENTCOM Fri; oil rebounds ~$103; Pakistan mediator; gas $3.956; ~$29.3B
Day 26 — 82nd Airborne deploying; 15-point ceasefire plan rejected; Brent −3.5% to $96.68; Pentagon $200B supplemental; CSIS replenishment $3.1B+; Speaker Johnson: 'almost done'; ~$30.5B

Day 27 — Israel kills IRGC Navy Cmdr Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas; ceasefire deadlocked; Brent surges to $105.85; 'wave of extensive strikes' on Isfahan arms factories; 82nd Airborne en route; Trump: 4-6 weeks; power plant deadline ~Mar 28; gas $3.98; ~$31.8B

Day 28 — Trump extends power plant deadline 10 days to Apr 6; G7 summit fractures; IDF overnight blitz Tehran/Kermanshah/Dezful ~70 bombs; CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets; Iran rejects 15-pt plan submits 5-pt counter; Brent $108.01; gas $3.98; in-person Islamabad talks pushed; ~$33.1B

Day 29 — BREAKING: Houthis launch first missile barrage at Israel — third front opens; Brent $112.57 (+4.22%); WTI briefly crosses $100; Dow enters correction; VIX 31.05; 300+ US wounded; 10 injured at Prince Sultan AB; Iran rejects ceasefire; Trump extends Hormuz deadline to Apr 6; Vance takes lead on negotiations; 82nd Airborne deploying; ~$34.3B

Day 30 — Islamabad quad summit convenes (Pakistan/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt); IRGC ultimatum on university campuses expires noon Mar 30; Israel strikes Arak heavy water + Ardakan yellowcake; CENTCOM: 11,000+ targets, 66% missile/drone production destroyed; 3,200+ 'No Kings' protests all 50 states; $200B supplemental faces bipartisan resistance; Kaine-Paul war powers fails 47-53; SPR 172M bbl exchange underway; ~$35.5B

Day 31 — Trump threatens to 'obliterate' power plants/oil wells/Kharg/desalination if no deal; Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations'; $23B arms sales bypass Congress; Houthis formally in war; Pakistan: both sides 'expressed confidence'; Brent $114.90+ (+51% in March — steepest monthly rise on record); 61% disapprove; 7,000-8,000 ground troops incoming; Spain closes airspace; Iraq PMF enters Iran; ~$36.8B

Day 32 — Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai — risks major oil spill; Brent $112.78 settle, nearing $120 on attack; gas crosses $4.00 nationally (highest since 2022); CA $5.88; diesel $5.42; 2 Chinese container ships transit Hormuz (first cargo since war began); Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint scenario; gold $4,578-4,600; 10Y volatile 4.31-4.43%; Moody's recession 49%; Islamabad peace talks stalled — 30-point gap; 61% disapprove; ~$38.1B

Day 33 — Trump claims Iran 'asked for CEASEFIRE' — markets surge; S&P +2.91%; Brent drops 5% to ~$105; 200 dynamic strikes in 24 hrs; IRGC cluster bomb hits Bnei Brak children; 3 missile waves at Israel; tanker Aqua 1 struck off Qatar; Kuwait airport fuel depots ablaze; IRGC threatens 17 US corporate HQs; Rubio: 'finish line coming'; Trump address 9 PM ET; gas $4.064; VLCC $423,736/day; 15 KIA / 348 WIA; Apr 6 deadline 5 days; ~$39.3B

Day 34 — S&P 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — dramatic intraday reversal on Iran-Oman transit protocol; first weekly gain (+3.4%); WTI $111.54 (+11.4%); Brent $109.03 (+7.8%); WTI-Brent inverted; gold $4,692.80; VIX 23.87; XOM -5.2%; CVX -4.6%; DAL $58.78; UAL 5% capacity cut; LMT $622.89 (+30% YTD); markets closed Apr 3 Good Friday; ~$40.6B

Day 35 — Good Friday — markets closed; Ford departs Split, resumes Eastern Med ops Day ~284; Bush CSG transiting Atlantic ETA Apr 10-12; 3 CSGs committed; Houthis resumed attacks on Israel but holding fire on shipping; Zarif publishes Foreign Affairs ceasefire proposal; IAEA severed from Iran since Feb 26; 972 lbs 60%-enriched uranium; PAC-3 tripling; gas $4.091; Apr 6 deadline 3 days; 28% approval; ~$41.8B

Day 36 — F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran (first US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003); pilot rescued, WSO missing; A-10 downed during rescue (pilot recovered); Pakistan ceasefire talks collapse — Iran refuses to meet; Houthis 4 attacks in 7 days; CMA CGM vessel first major Western transit of Hormuz; French transit followed by Japanese tanker; Trump approval 35-39.7%; 61% disapprove handling; jobs +178K (3x expected); CPI approaching 4%; Apr 6 deadline 2 days; gas $4.091; ~$43.1B

Day 37 — WSO rescued after 48+ hours evading IRGC inside Iran — CIA deception campaign + makeshift airbase on Iranian soil; 2 C-130s intentionally destroyed; April 6 ultimatum <24 hours; Trump: 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day'; Iran BMs at IDF HQ Tel Aviv + Neot Hovav (3rd hit); Mahshahr petrochemical zone struck (5 killed, 170+ injured); 82nd Airborne 2,000-3,000 deploying; Hezbollah rockets 60km into Israel; 5th Houthi missile intercepted; ceasefire 2% by Apr 7; Moody's recession 49%; AG Bondi fired; gas $4.104; ~$44.3B

Day 38 — Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz — threatens strikes on power plants and bridges; 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egyptian-Pakistani-Turkish mediators rejected by Tehran; OPEC+ agrees to increase output 206K bpd for May (dismissed as 'academic'); Trump admits arming Iranian Kurdish opposition groups; 53 transits last week (up from 36); Brent ~$111.25; WTI ~$111.81; gas $4.119 (AAA); markets reopen Mon after Easter; S&P futures ~6,631-6,647; VIX pre-market 24.48-24.88; gold ~$4,665-4,677; ~$45.6B

Day 39 — US strikes 50+ military targets on Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports); Israel launches wide-scale bridge/railway strikes (Qom, Kashan, Tabriz-Zanjan, Karaj); 18 civilians killed in Alborz Province; Tehran synagogue destroyed; Sharif University AI data center hit; Asaluyeh petrochemical complex struck; yellowcake facility in Ardakan hit; 3 Tehran airports struck; IRGC Maj. Gen. Khademi (intelligence chief) + Asghar Bakeri (Quds Force) killed; Iran rejected ceasefire but submitted 10-point counter-proposal; Vance: 'military goals achieved'; Trump: 'a whole civilisation will die tonight'; WTI spiking to $115; S&P ~6,588 (down 0.5-0.7%); 8 PM ET deadline — binary inflection point; ~$46.9B

Day 40 — CEASEFIRE RALLY CLOSE: S&P 6,782.81 (+2.51%), Dow 47,909.92 (+2.85% best day since Apr 2025), Nasdaq 22,635.00 (+2.80%); Brent closes $94.75 (-14%); WTI $94.41 (-16%, biggest drop since Apr 2020); VIX 21.04 (-18.39%); gold $4,799-4,867; XOM -4.8%, CVX -4.35%, UAL +7.84%, LMT -0.77%, RTX +2.40%; Iran re-closes Hormuz afternoon after Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' kills 254 in Lebanon (50 jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets in 10 min); Netanyahu confirms Lebanon excluded from ceasefire; European leaders demand inclusion; only 5 bulk carriers outbound through Hormuz by noon; CENTCOM: 13,000+ targets cumulative, 80% air defenses destroyed, every Iranian sub sunk; ~$47.7B

Day 41 — CEASEFIRE FRACTURING: Brent rebounds to ~$97-98; WTI to ~$97-98 (Bloomberg: 'stocks dropped and oil advanced as traders watched to see if the US-Iran ceasefire holds'); S&P reverses intraday to ~6,752.64 from 6,782.81 close; VIX climbs toward 23-26; $12-18 Hormuz risk premium re-embedded; Soufan Center: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse'; Hezbollah launches retaliatory missiles on northern Israel early Apr 9; White House confirms Vance + Witkoff + Kushner for Islamabad talks Apr 10-11 (highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979); Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12 — dual-carrier capability; Trump tells CNBC forces ready for 'next conquest'; Pope Leo XIV Apr 11 vigil; Lebanon declares Apr 9 national day of mourning; ~$48.5B

Day 42 — CEASEFIRE DAY 3 — CRITICAL 48 HOURS: VP Vance departs for Islamabad with Witkoff + Kushner — highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979; formal talks scheduled Saturday Apr 12 local; Iran delegation led by Ghalibaf + Araghchi; former FM Kharazi dies from Apr 1 airstrike wounds (age 81); Israel conducts 50+ strikes in Lebanon in 24 hrs (1,888+ killed total, 6,092 injured) with 'marked reduction' in Beirut targeting due to US pressure; Trump asked Netanyahu to be 'more low-key'; Hezbollah resumed rocket fire at Kiryat Shmona, Metula, Misgav Am; ADNOC CEO: 'Strait of Hormuz is not open'; only ~15 ships in 48 hrs vs 110/day pre-war; 325 loaded tankers stranded (Lloyd's); March CPI released — energy-driven inflation; Brent ~$96; WTI ~$98; gas $4.166; S&P 6,825 (Apr 9 close +0.62%); VIX ~19.4-21.0; CSIS: ~$30B total; Harvard: $2B/day, $1T long-term; WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer); supplemental shrunk $200B→$80-100B; 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29; Congress returns next week; Iran says 3 of 10 ceasefire points violated; Trump: 'Shootin Starts' if no REAL AGREEMENT; Pope vigil tomorrow; Bush CSG ETA today-Sunday; ~$49.25B

Day 43 — ISLAMABAD TALKS BEGIN — FIRST US-IRAN FACE-TO-FACE SINCE 1979: VP Vance, Witkoff, Kushner meet Ghalibaf + Araghchi at Serena Hotel — Pakistan's Sharif and FM Asim Munir mediate; ceasefire holds ~96 hours on Iran front; Iran claims US agreed to release frozen assets — US flatly denies; Ghalibaf carries blood-stained bags to talks; Lebanon excluded — Israel strikes 120 Hezbollah sites in 24 hrs (14 killed Apr 10 incl. 13 State Security in Nabatieh); Hezbollah fires 30+ rockets at northern Israel; 'Tehran Tollbooth' — IRGC charges up to $2M/vessel via Larak Island corridor, caps at 15 ships/day; 500-700 vessels stranded (Lloyd's); war risk insurance 8x pre-war; dated Brent $131.97 vs futures $96.66 ($35 premium = physical scarcity); S&P best week since Nov (+3.6%); March CPI 3.3% YoY — gas +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967); UMich sentiment 47.6 all-time low; Pope Leo XIV peace vigil at St. Peter's; Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea Apr 14-18; LMT $4.7B PAC-3 contract; CNN intelligence: China preparing air defense delivery to Iran; 60-day War Powers deadline 18 days away; ~$50.05B

Day 44 — ISLAMABAD TALKS COLLAPSE AFTER 21 HOURS — TRUMP DECLARES US NAVY BLOCKADE OF HORMUZ: Talks end pre-dawn Apr 12 with no agreement; Vance press conference: 'We have not reached an agreement' — identifies nuclear as core sticking point, leaves 'final and best offer'; Ghalibaf five-point X post blames US: 'the other side was ultimately unable to gain the trust of the Iranian delegation'; five major gaps: nuclear enrichment, Hormuz control, Lebanon inclusion, sanctions/frozen assets, war reparations; Turkish media: Araghchi and Witkoff 'nearly came to blows' during Hormuz discussion; Iranian delegation arrived dressed in black carrying shoes and bloodied belongings of Minab schoolchildren; Trump Truth Social: 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz' — orders Navy to interdict every vessel that paid Iran's toll, destroy Iranian mines, warns 'Any Iranian who fires at us, or at peaceful vessels, will be BLOWN TO HELL!'; Trump: 'fully LOCKED AND LOADED, and our Military will finish up the little that is left of Iran!'; Iran's deputy parliament speaker Nikzad: US 'learned that the victorious side is determined by the will of nations... not by rhetoric on social media'; ceasefire on Iran front technically holds but future uncertain — expires ~Apr 22; Vance boarded Air Force Two 7:08 AM Islamabad local; Pope Leo XIV Apr 12: 'closeness to the people of Lebanon'; Netanyahu: Israel struck 200+ Hezbollah targets over weekend; Lebanon cumulative: 2,020+ killed, 6,436+ wounded, 1.2M displaced; Lebanon-Israel talks Apr 15 at State Dept (first contact since 1983); PM Salam postpones Washington trip; markets closed weekend — reopen Monday under heavy reversal pressure; Ford deployment at 292 days — post-Vietnam record breaks ~Apr 14; Bush CSG approaching/at Gibraltar; Congress returns Apr 14; Schumer announces Senate WPR vote; 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29; ~$50.85B

Day 45 — BLOCKADE EFFECTIVE 14:00 GMT — IRGC WARNS OF 'HARSH AND DECISIVE' FORCE: Trump's US naval blockade of Iranian ports takes effect; CENTCOM narrows scope to Iranian ports only (not all Hormuz traffic) — significant divergence from Trump's Truth Social announcement; IRGC declares any military vessel approaching the Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force; IRGC: 'Security in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman is either for everyone or for NO ONE' / 'NO PORT in the region will be safe'; Mohsen Rezaei: Iran has 'major untouched levers' — will not be coerced by 'tweets and imaginary plans'; Ghalibaf posts Washington gas station prices, taunts: 'Enjoy the current pump figures... Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas'; UK explicitly declines to participate — Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war'; Australia not asked; Iran's armed forces call blockade 'an illegal act amounting to piracy'; ceasefire holds ~144 hrs on Iran front — Trump: 'holding well,' 'their military is destroyed, their whole navy is under water'; oil breaks $100 — Brent ~$103 (+7.3%), WTI $97-105 intraday; S&P ~6,775-6,781 (-0.5-0.6%); VIX 21.30 (+10.8%); Gold $4,724 (-0.6%); gas $4.125/gal (CA $5.89, OK $3.27); Fed Mar CPI 3.3% YoY, +0.9% MoM (steepest since mid-2022); 10Y 4.317%; Bitcoin $70,900 (-2.7%); ExxonMobil $157.61 (+3.3%), CVX $192.39 (+2.0%); WSJ: 60%+ IRGC naval fleet tasked with Hormuz still intact — contradicts White House 97% mine elimination claim; Atlantic Council: 63-71% AWACS fleet deployed, 29-43% THAAD; 16 MQ-9 Reapers lost since Epic Fury; Pentagon requests ~$200B supplemental — 71% of Americans oppose; Bush CSG at/approaching Gibraltar; Ford deployment breaks post-Vietnam record (294 days, Lincoln 2019-20) ~Apr 14; 82nd Airborne remains forward-deployed; CENTCOM: 'additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort'; Lebanon cumulative (Public Health EOC/CGTN): 2,055 killed, 6,588 wounded; IDF captures Bint Jbeil — 100+ Hezbollah operatives killed; Red Cross paramedic killed by IDF drone; Katz announces Operation 'Silver Plough' to demolish border homes; Pope Leo XIV departs Rome for 11-day Africa trip — pushes back on Trump's Apr 12 attacks: 'Blessed are the peacemakers'; EU's von der Leyen: EU fossil fuel import costs +€22B in 44 days; Spain's defense minister calls blockade 'one more episode in this whole downward spiral'; ceasefire expires ~Apr 21; 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29 — 16 days; ~$51.75B

Day 46 — BLOCKADE FIRST FULL DAY — IEA: LARGEST OIL SUPPLY DISRUPTION IN HISTORY — OIL RETREATS ON DIPLOMATIC HOPES: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~168 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); only 3 commercial vessels transit Hormuz — Rich Starry (Chinese-owned Malawi-flagged US-sanctioned 250K barrels methanol, initially turned back at Qeshm Island then passed overnight), Murlikishan (US-sanctioned), Peace Gulf (Panama-flagged to UAE) — none heading to Iranian ports, blockade NOT directly tested; IEA April OMR confirms 10.1 mb/d March loss to 97 mb/d — Hormuz throughput 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline); IEA Birol: 'Worse than 1973, 1979, and 2022 combined'; steepest quarterly demand decline since COVID projected; oil retreats on diplomatic optimism: WTI $95.94 (-3.2%), Brent $97.20 (-2.2%); S&P 500 ~6,917 (+0.4%) ERASES ALL WAR LOSSES since Feb 28; Nasdaq 10th consecutive up day; VIX 18.4 (-3.9%) lowest since war began; US-Iran 'discussing details for potential second in-person meeting' (CNN); Pakistan PM Sharif + Army Chief Munir working to host Round 2 Islamabad before Apr 22 expiration; thousands rally in Tehran against US blockade — pro-government demonstration accusing US of piracy; Congress returns from 2-week recess — Democrats prepare war powers votes; key swing Rs: Collins (ME), Curtis (UT), Paul (KY, co-sponsoring Dem measure); Rep.-elect Clay Fuller (R-GA replacing expelled MTG) to be sworn in giving Speaker Johnson 2-vote margin; DHS partial government shutdown enters Day 66 — longest in US history; Battle of Bint Jbeil continues (Day 5-6): IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of the stadium — site of Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech — 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed, dozens remain active; IDF raided hospital where armed Hezbollah operatives were sheltering; Hezbollah SecGen Qassem rejects Apr 15 Israel-Lebanon talks as 'pointless' and 'a free concession'; China calls blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible,' demands comprehensive ceasefire; France announces preparations for 'peaceful multinational mission aimed at restoring freedom of navigation' in Hormuz — separate from US belligerent operations; ICRC center struck in Tyre (one killed); 2 IDF soldiers moderately wounded + 6 lightly wounded by Hezbollah drone in southern Lebanon Apr 13; woman injured Nahariya by Hezbollah rocket Apr 13; Lebanon cumulative: 2,089+ killed (+34), 6,762+ wounded (+174); 13 US KIA / 381 WIA unchanged (CENTCOM); Pentagon $200B supplemental remains pending; CSIS: ~$30B consensus; AEI: $22-31B; Harvard/Bilmes: $1T long-term; WarCosts.org: ~$35B; THAAD:Shahed cost ratio 363:1 ($12.7M vs $35K); WSJ reports Trump/advisers considering resuming limited military strikes on Iran as leverage — NOT implemented; 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29 — 15 days; ~$52.55B

Day 47 — S&P BREACHES 7,000 ATH — IRAN THREATENS PERSIAN GULF + SEA OF OMAN + RED SEA COUNTER-BLOCKADE — BLOOMBERG: 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE EXTENSION UNDER CONSIDERATION — PAKISTAN ARMY CHIEF ARRIVES TEHRAN: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~192 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); blockade enters Day 3 — CENTCOM 48-hour update: 9 vessels total turned back, no breaches claimed; USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) active combat ops; 27 Navy vessels in CENTCOM theater (~41% of deployed/underway ships globally), 16,500+ sailors/Marines — highest concentration since 2003 Iraq invasion; IRGC Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi THREATENS counter-blockade of Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea on state TV — 'will not allow any exports or imports to continue'; Bloomberg 15:12 UTC: US and Iran reportedly considering 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE EXTENSION (pre-Apr 21 expiration); Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrives in Tehran with 'new message from Washington'; Pakistan PM Sharif departs on 4-day tour to Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Türkiye; Trump told NY Post Apr 14: talks 'could be happening over next two days'; Trump Apr 15: war 'very close to over,' Iran's blockade response 'pretty amazing'; S&P 500 new intraday ATH 7,003.71 — first time above 7,000 surpassing Jan 28 record of 7,002.28; Nasdaq +0.9-1.0% to ~23,852-23,875 — 10th consecutive positive session; VIX 17.74-18.36 (-3-5%); Dow -48-97 pts (-0.1-0.2%); Bitcoin $74,068-74,828 (8-day rally); WTI $90.90-93.73 mixed; Brent ~$95-96.30 slightly higher; gold $4,830.31 (-0.24%); DXY 97.85 (6-week low); XOM $148.78 (-2.53%), CVX $186.54 (-2.73%) war premium unwinding; LMT $609-617 (AH $612.28); Bank of America Q1 EPS $1.11 Rev $30.3B (+7% YoY) stock +2.5%; Morgan Stanley Q1 RECORD EPS $3.43 RECORD Rev $20.58B ROTCE 27.1% stock +5%+; ASML beat Q1 raised FY guidance $42.4-47.2B on AI demand; gas $4.108/gal (-1 cent); Treasury Sec Bessent Apr 15: 'optimistic' about $3 gas by June 20-Sept 20; Israel-Lebanon direct talks Apr 14 (first since 1983 at State Dept) — Israel refuses ceasefire commitment, presses Hezbollah disarmament; Hezbollah launches ~30 rockets at northern Israel Apr 15; Hezbollah claimed 24 separate attacks Apr 14 on northern Israel + IDF positions in S. Lebanon; shot down Israeli Hermes 450 drone; Qassem calls Washington talks 'futile,' 'free concession'; Battle of Bint Jbeil nearing conclusion — IDF claims near-complete capture; 100+ Hezbollah killed; 1 IDF soldier killed, 13 wounded Apr 14; 10 paratroopers wounded (3 severely); 3 Radwan Force fighters captured; Operation 'Silver Plough' demolishing border villages; 5 IDF divisions operating (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd); Lebanon cumulative Apr 14: 2,089 killed (EOC) or 2,124 (TASS Health Min later update), 6,762 wounded, 168 children, 254 women, 87-91 health workers; +43 Lebanese killed in past 24 hrs per manifest; 13 US KIA / 381 WIA unchanged (CENTCOM); Intercept: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries; Iran cumulative: Health Min 2,076+, HRANA 3,636, Hengaw 6,620 military, Iran Intl 4,700+, Iran Red Crescent ~2,000 fatalities/27,000 injuries; IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast to 3.1%; Iran projected contract 6.1%, Qatar -8.6%, Iraq -6.8%; Senate War Powers vote 2 PM ET Apr 15 expected fail — 60-day WPR clock expires Apr 29-May 1; Pentagon $200B supplemental pending; CNN Apr 14: satellite imagery shows Iran actively RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during ceasefire — equipment at blocked tunnel entrances Khomeyn + south of Tabriz; US intel: ~50% Iran missile launchers survived first 40 days; CNN Apr 11: China preparing to deliver new air defense systems to Iran within weeks; CENTCOM Apr 14: 6 vessels turned back no breaches; CENTCOM Apr 15: 9 total vessels turned back; WSJ: US intercepted 8 oil tankers entering/leaving Iranian ports; Reuters: US destroyer interdicted 2 oil tankers from Chabahar via radio; Kpler: 5 laden product tankers holding stationary in Gulf of Oman; Rich Starry reversed course; UK Starmer: will NOT join blockade; France/UK co-host Friday summit on multilateral freedom-of-navigation mission; China 'strongly condemned' blockade; Qatar rejected 'any escalatory moves'; Harvard Bilmes CNBC Apr 14: ~$2B/day active fighting, certain will reach $1 trillion long-term; AEI Day 33: ~$50B total economic impact; Iran-cost-ticker Day 47: ~$52-53B; Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact; CPI March 3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM) gas +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967); Ford at record 9+ month deployment; Fed meets Apr 28-29 markets price ~no cut; ~$53.35B cumulative

Day 48 — S&P RECORD CLOSE 7,022.95 / NEW INTRADAY ATH 7,040.03 — SENATE WPR FAILS 47-52 (4TH TIME) — LEBANON TOLL 2,167/7,061 — CEASEFIRE ~216 HRS / BLOCKADE DAY 4: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~216 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); blockade Day 4 — CENTCOM 72-hr tally: 13-14 vessels turned back, no breaches claimed; USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in Gulf of Oman Apr 14 (Stars & Stripes); BBC Verify/MarineTraffic disputes CENTCOM's 'zero penetrations' — at least 4 Iran-related transits Apr 14, Rich Starry U-turned at Gulf of Oman, Elpis stopped near Iranian coast, Agios Fanourios I became first crude carrier heading west post-blockade (gCaptain); Hormuz transit volume below 10% of pre-war average; Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth (Pentagon Apr 16): US 'LOCKED AND LOADED' to restart combat if talks collapse; WSJ: Trump weighing 'resumption of limited military strikes' to break Islamabad stalemate; No new US strikes on Iran — Epic Fury kinetic campaign remains paused per Adm. Brad Cooper; Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir received by FM Araghchi Apr 15 and met Speaker Ghalibaf morning Apr 16 carrying 'new message from Washington' (contents undisclosed); Ghalibaf phoned Lebanese Speaker Berri saying Lebanon ceasefire 'as important as' Iran's; PM Shehbaz Sharif began 4-day tour (Jeddah → Doha → Antalya Diplomacy Forum with Erdogan Apr 15-18); MBS met Sharif 2+ hrs Apr 15 in Jeddah; Saudi pledged $3B to Pakistan reserves + extended existing $5B deposit; Round 2 Islamabad-or-Geneva likely next week per Pakistani sources; WH spox Leavitt Apr 15 pushed back on CNN ceasefire-extension scoop as 'bad reporting'; Trump (Fox Business Apr 15): war 'very close to being over'; Iran FM spox Baghaei Apr 15 confirmed messages flowing via Pakistan; Senate REJECTED War Powers Resolution 47-52 Apr 15 — 4th defeat of Kaine-led measure; Rand Paul (R-KY) sole GOP yea, Fetterman (D-PA) sole Dem nay, Jim Justice (R-WV) did not vote; Blumenthal pledged to re-file 'next week'; Thune 'feel pretty good' about military gains but needs 'plan to wind this down'; Hawley wants 60-day AUMF vote; Curtis won't back further war funding without authorization; Murkowski drafting alternative; Collins: presidential power 'is not unlimited'; 60-day War Powers Act deadline falls ~May 1 setting up next inflection with appropriations; Trump Truth Social Apr 14-15 claimed Iran 'killed at least 42,000 innocent, completely unarmed, protesters in the last two months' — NBC flagged unsourced (HRANA ~7,000, Iran govt 3,100+); Trump told WaPo Apr 15 'China agreed not to send weapons to Iran' (Beijing denied); Trump Apr 15 Fox claim UK + 'a couple of other countries' sending minesweepers — UK government EXPLICITLY told BBC it will not assist the blockade; Trump floated unconfirmed 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Apr 16 (NBC) — not confirmed by either party; Rubio-Aoun phone call Apr 16 on Lebanon ceasefire; Treasury Sec Bessent Apr 15 announced secondary sanctions as 'financial equivalent' of kinetic ops; designated 3 individuals, 17 entities, 9 oil tankers tied to Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani; halt on 30-day waivers for Iranian/Russian oil; Bessent forecast $3 gas by June 20-Sept 20; Pope Leo XIV remains in Algeria (Apr 13-14 trip); no fresh Vatican statement Apr 15-16; 19 UN Human Rights experts Apr 15 called Israel's Apr 8 Lebanon strikes 'blatant violation of UN Charter' flagging potential crimes against humanity; IAEA DG Grossi (Seoul Apr 15) insisted strict international checks must anchor any US-Iran deal; China MFA called blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible'; Lavrov (Beijing) 'no military solution'; Merz (Germany) warned Israel's Lebanon campaign 'could cause the failure of the peace process as a whole'; Pezeshkian (Tehran Emergency Org Apr 15): 'No power in the world can force the Iranian nation into submission'; Araghchi (X) talks were 'inches away' before 'maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade'; CNN analysis Apr 15 flagged possible Mojtaba Khamenei incapacitation (no public appearance); Israeli security cabinet Apr 15 debated proposed 1-week Lebanon ceasefire — ended without decision; Netanyahu (recorded): Israel 'prepared for possibility fighting with Iran could resume' and will strike Hezbollah 'wherever required'; First direct Israeli-Lebanese ambassador talks in decades Apr 14 in Washington, mediated by Rubio; Hezbollah MP Hussein Hajj Hassan condemned talks as 'a grave sin'; Hezbollah rockets Apr 15 wounded 5 IDF soldiers (1 serious, 4 light); Apr 16 IDF Nabatieh strike kills 19 incl. 13 Lebanese State Security; last functioning Tebnin hospital reportedly struck Apr 15; Hezbollah Apr 16 damaged 1,500-yr-old church in Nahariya, missile at Ashdod (claimed Ashdod Naval Base);
